tagline: Duolingo-class Dutch learning PWA with AI roleplay boss-fights for situational practice
vision: Most adult Dutch learners pick up vocab and grammar from apps like Duolingo, then freeze the moment they need to actually use it - at work, with a landlord, ordering coffee. Lekkertaal closes that gap: daily 5-minute drills build the foundation, then per-unit boss-fight roleplays force you to apply it in realistic scenarios with an AI playing the other side. Streaks, coins, badges keep you coming back. Mascot is Stroop the stroopwafel.

deploy.stack_canon: TanStack Start + Cloudflare Workers + D1 + Drizzle + shadcn/ui + Clerk + Vercel AI SDK + ElevenLabs + Resend + PostHog + Sentry + UptimeRobot

key_decisions.auth: Clerk open signup (Google + email). No cohort, single global namespace. Public-by-default.
key_decisions.content_frame: Generic CEFR-levelled (A1/A2/B1) curriculum. Origin invisible. v0 ships A2 only.
key_decisions.structure: Linear path + sandbox unlock per completed unit. Duolingo's pattern.
key_decisions.drill_types: match-pairs, multiple-choice, translation-typing, fill-in-the-blank, word-ordering, listening-mc
key_decisions.roleplay_scoring: In-character during chat, 5-rubric scorecard after (grammar/vocab/task/fluency/politeness, 1-5 stars each). Errors feed into spaced-rep queue (SM-2). No fail state, best-attempt sticks.
key_decisions.ai_provider: Vercel AI SDK. Claude Sonnet 4.6 primary (via @ai-sdk/anthropic), OpenAI gpt-4o-mini fallback (via @ai-sdk/openai), Gemini 2.5 Flash for image gen (via @ai-sdk/google).
key_decisions.tts: ElevenLabs primary. OpenAI TTS fallback. Audio cached in R2.
key_decisions.gamification: Streaks + XP + coins + freezes + badges + global leaderboard. NO hearts. NO league system.
key_decisions.social: Global leaderboard (today/week/all-time) + public profiles + users directory. No friends/follow in v0.
key_decisions.notifications: Web push (daily nag) + email (weekly digest + streak recovery). No WhatsApp.
key_decisions.visuals: Cutesy. Fredoka (display) + Nunito (body). Dutch palette: orange #FF6B1A, canal blue #1F6FB2, tulip pink #FF7AA2, warm paper bg. shadcn customized: rounded-3xl, chunky 3D button shadows, Motion whileTap.
key_decisions.mascot: Stroop the stroopwafel via Rive (state machine: idle/happy/proud/concerned/sleeping). Concept art via Nano Banana 2.
key_decisions.ui_animation: Motion (motion/react import). LazyMotion + m components for SSR-safe.
key_decisions.sfx: 4 hero SFX: correct-ding, wrong-buzz, lesson-complete fanfare, streak-fire whoosh. Via /ro:sfx-elevenlabs.

personas: Working adults learning Dutch in the Netherlands (primary)
Yellowtail Dutch Group 4 users (Ronan, Mehmet, Judith, Michiel) seeded day-one
out_of_scope_v0: Real-time voice / OpenAI Realtime / Gemini Live (Phase 3)
A1 + B1 starter content (Phase 2; v0 ships A2 only)

phases.title: Phase 1 — v0 MVP
phases.goal: A new user can sign up, take placement, get routed to A2 Unit 4, complete a lesson (6 drills, ~5min), earn XP + streak, see Stroop celebrate. After completing all Unit 4 lessons, unlock the Unit 4 boss-fight roleplay, complete it, get a 5-rubric scorecard with errors-into-spaced-rep, earn a badge. Tomorrow at 8pm get a push notification if they haven't done today's lesson. Sunday get an email digest. All 4 seeded users visible on leaderboard and as public profiles.

stories.title: Ingest course content from llm-wiki vault into structured seed JSON
stories.ears: When the seed pipeline runs, the system shall read the WhatsApp chat transcript (raw/whatsapp/_chat.txt), the 73 notepad photos, and the 4 course PDFs from /Users/ronan/Downloads-Keep/learn-dutch/12th\ may/, OCR / parse them via Claude or Gemini vision, and emit structured JSON for seeding: seed/{units.json, vocab.json, exercises.json, scenarios.json}.
acceptance: Reads from /Users/ronan/Downloads-Keep/learn-dutch/12th\ may/WhatsApp\ Chat\ -\ Yellowtail\ Dutch\ Group/ and /Users/ronan/Downloads-Keep/learn-dutch/12th\ may/notepad\ photos/
At least 80% of vocab from notepad photos appears in seed/vocab.json
Photos that fail OCR are flagged in seed/ingest-errors.txt
Idempotent: re-running produces the same seed (deterministic ordering, sha-keyed dedupe)
Each vocab item has: nl, en, example_sentence_nl, example_sentence_en, source_image_path, cefr_level
Each unit-level grouping covers one Hoofdstuk or thematic block
Run via: pnpm seed:ingest --source /path/to/zip-or-folder

stories.title: Finalise TanStack Start + CF Workers scaffold
stories.ears: When pnpm dev runs, the system shall start a TanStack Start dev server with Cloudflare adapter wired and live-reload working.
acceptance: wrangler.jsonc has name='lekkertaal', compatibility_date current
vite.config.ts has @cloudflare/vite-plugin + tanstackStart + viteReact + tailwindcss
pnpm dev starts cleanly on http://localhost:3000
pnpm build outputs a valid CF Worker bundle
Project README updated with stack overview

stories.title: Drizzle schema + D1 migration + seed loader
stories.ears: When pnpm db:push runs against the D1 database, the system shall create all tables from src/db/schema.ts. When pnpm seed:load runs, the system shall populate the DB from seed/*.json.
acceptance: src/db/schema.ts defines: users, courses, units, lessons, exercises, vocab, grammar_concepts, scenarios, user_lesson_progress, user_unit_progress, roleplay_sessions, roleplay_errors, spaced_rep_queue, xp_events, coin_events, daily_completions, badges, user_badges, push_subscriptions, notification_log
All foreign keys + indexes for hot query paths (leaderboard, user-current-unit, due-spaced-rep)
drizzle-kit configured with dialect=sqlite, driver=d1-http
pnpm db:generate creates migration files in drizzle/
pnpm db:push applies cleanly to local D1 (--local) and remote D1 (--remote)
pnpm seed:load reads seed/*.json and INSERT-OR-IGNORES rows (idempotent)
Uses --local flag for development; deploy story wires CF_ACCOUNT_ID to push remote

stories.title: Clerk auth: open signup with Google + email
stories.ears: When a new user clicks sign up, the system shall offer Google OAuth and email-magic-link via Clerk. When sign up completes, a Clerk webhook syncs the new user into users table.
acceptance: pnpm add @clerk/tanstack-react-start
ClerkProvider wraps the app in __root.tsx
.dev.vars contains CLERK_SECRET_KEY + VITE_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY (from ~/.claude/.env CLERK_SECRET_KEY_LEKKERTAAL + VITE_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY_LEKKERTAAL)
/sign-in and /sign-up routes use Clerk's hosted UI
Server function /api/auth/webhook receives Clerk user.created event and inserts into users table (handles signed payload verification)
Existing Clerk users seamlessly sign in
Sign out works

stories.title: Placement test (5 questions) + level picker fallback
stories.ears: When a user signs up for the first time, the system shall present a 5-question placement test covering A1-to-B1 difficulty. When the user answers all 5, the system shall route them to the appropriate starting unit. The user may bypass via 'I know my level' link to pick A1/A2/B1 manually.
acceptance: /onboarding route with placement test UI
5 questions hardcoded in src/data/placement-test.ts: Q1=A1 vocab, Q2=A2-early perfectum, Q3=A2-late word-order drag-tiles, Q4=B1-early subordinate-clause, Q5=B1-late idiom comprehension
Each correct answer moves user up a sub-level; final score sets users.cefr_level
'I know my level' link bypasses to /onboarding/level-pick with A1/A2/B1 buttons
Both paths land on /app/path at the right starting unit
Skippable for users who have already completed (idempotent)

stories.title: Reminder-hour + push-permission onboarding
stories.ears: When a user completes the placement test, the system shall ask them to choose a daily reminder hour and grant web push permission. Both are skippable.
acceptance: /onboarding/notifications route
Reminder hour picker: default 20:00 in user's timezone (auto-detected via Intl.DateTimeFormat)
users.reminder_hour, users.timezone, users.reminder_enabled stored
Web Push permission request fires only when user clicks 'Enable reminders'
On grant, subscription saved to push_subscriptions table
Skip link routes to /app/path with reminders disabled

stories.title: Path view: linear unit tree visualisation (/app/path)
stories.ears: When a signed-in user navigates to /app/path, the system shall render the unit tree for their CEFR level with completed/current/locked status per unit. When a user taps a non-locked unit, the system shall navigate to its detail page.
acceptance: Server function loads units for user.cefr_level + user_unit_progress
Vertical scrollable path UI; each unit is a Stroop-themed badge
Status indicator: green-completed, orange-in-progress, grey-locked
Tap on unlocked unit -> /app/unit/:slug
Mobile-first responsive design
Motion animation on unit-status transition (completion celebration)
Top bar always visible: streak / xp / coins counters

stories.title: Unit detail page (/app/unit/:slug)
stories.ears: When the user navigates to a unit's detail page, the system shall show the lessons list, the unit's vocab grid, the unit's grammar concept (if any), and a boss-fight CTA (locked until lessons complete).
acceptance: Server-function load: unit + lessons + vocab + grammar_concept
Lessons list with per-lesson completion + best score
Vocab grid (preview of ~10 words; 'Show all' opens modal with full list)
Grammar concept card (collapsible Rule + Examples)
Boss-fight CTA card: locked-greyed if not all lessons done, unlocked-glowing if all done
Sticky bottom: 'Start next lesson' button (always visible)

stories.title: Lesson player (/app/lesson/:id)
stories.ears: When a user starts a lesson, the system shall render the lesson's drills sequentially. On all drills correct, the system shall navigate to a completion celebration screen with XP awarded and Stroop animation.
acceptance: Progress bar at top, advances per drill
Each drill type renders via a per-type component (US-010..US-015)
On drill answer: immediate correct/wrong feedback + transition to next drill
Wrong answers add the item to spaced_rep_queue
On lesson complete: /app/lesson/:id/complete with +XP + Stroop happy animation + 'Back to path'
Skip lesson option (with confirm dialog)
Keyboard-navigable (Enter to submit, Tab to navigate)

stories.title: Drill: Match pairs (tap NL <-> EN)
stories.ears: When a match-pairs drill loads, the system shall display 4 Dutch tiles and 4 English tiles. When the user taps an NL tile then an EN tile, the system shall confirm or reject the match.
acceptance: 4 NL tiles + 4 EN tiles, shuffled positions
Tap NL -> highlight; tap EN -> confirm match (correct = green flash + remove pair) or reject (red flash + 100ms shake)
Drill complete when all 4 pairs matched
Audio play option per tile (tap speaker icon -> ElevenLabs TTS via US-029)
Accessibility: full keyboard nav with arrow keys + Enter to confirm

stories.title: Drill: Multiple choice + Listening MC
stories.ears: When a multiple-choice drill loads, the system shall present a prompt (text or audio) and 4 options. When the user taps an option, the system shall confirm or reject.
acceptance: Text MC: prompt as text, 4 options as buttons
Listening MC: 🔊 button plays ElevenLabs audio of Dutch sentence; user picks meaning from 4 options
Selected option immediately styled correct/wrong
Brief explanation on wrong: 'X is correct because Y'
Audio plays via cached R2 URL (US-029)
One component file handles both variants via a `mode: 'text' | 'audio'` prop

stories.title: Drill: Translation typing (EN -> NL)
stories.ears: When a translation-typing drill loads, the system shall show an English sentence and a text input. When the user submits, the system shall grade with Levenshtein tolerance.
acceptance: Input field with mobile-friendly Dutch keyboard hint
Levenshtein distance <=1 = correct (allows one typo)
Case-insensitive, punctuation-tolerant grading
On submit: show user's answer + canonical answer side-by-side
Speaker icon to play canonical answer via ElevenLabs
Shake animation on wrong
Hint button (costs 5 coins): reveal first 2 letters

stories.title: Drill: Fill-in-the-blank
stories.ears: When a fill-in-the-blank drill loads, the system shall present a sentence with one word blanked out. The user types the missing word.
acceptance: Sentence with ___ where the blank is; rest visible
Input field inline-flush with the sentence
Common grammar drills: verb conjugation (Ik ___ gegaan), articles (___ huis), prepositions (Ik ga ___ school)
Levenshtein <=1 grading like US-012
Hint button reveals first letter (10 coins)

stories.title: Drill: Word ordering (drag tiles)
stories.ears: When a word-ordering drill loads, the system shall present scrambled word tiles. The user taps tiles in order to build a sentence. Submit grades against canonical order.
acceptance: Tiles pool at bottom + sentence-building area at top
Tap a tile -> moves to sentence area in tap-order; tap again -> returns to pool
Touch-friendly drag option for tablet/phone
Submit when all tiles placed; grades against canonical order (the Levenshtein-tolerant comparison handles equivalents like vandaag vs op vandaag)
Used heavily for V2 word-order practice and perfectum split
Speaker icon plays canonical sentence

stories.title: Drill: Listening MC handled in US-011 (consolidated)
stories.ears: When US-011 includes the listening mode, US-015 is a no-op tracking stub for the rubric.
acceptance: Listening MC scope folded into US-011's component (mode='audio')
This story is closed with a 1-line note in progress.txt confirming the consolidation

stories.title: Scenario library: 8 A2 scenarios seeded
stories.ears: When the DB is seeded, the system shall include 8 A2 roleplay scenarios, one per unit boss-fight, each with NPC persona + opening line + must-use vocab + success criteria.
acceptance: 8 scenarios in seed/scenarios.json: cafe-ordering-coffee, weekend-recap-with-friend, doctor-appointment, supermarket-find-it, work-standup, haircut, landlord-leak, asking-directions

Each scenario has: slug, unit_id, title_nl, title_en, difficulty, npc_name, npc_persona, npc_voice_id (ElevenLabs), opening_nl, must_use_vocab[], must_use_grammar[], success_criteria (3-5 bullets), failure_modes (2-3 bullets), estimated_minutes, xp_reward (100 base), badge_unlock
Each linked to a specific A2 unit's boss-fight
Validated against seed/scenarios.schema.json

stories.title: Roleplay chat UI (/app/scenario/:slug)
stories.ears: When a user enters a roleplay scenario, the system shall open a chat where the user types Dutch and Claude replies in character as the NPC. When 8 user turns elapse OR the user types 'klaar', the system shall end the conversation and route to the scorecard.
acceptance: Server-function /api/roleplay/:slug/stream returns Claude streamText with NPC system prompt
Streaming UI: user types Dutch, AI streams response in chat-bubble style
Every AI Dutch message has a 🔊 tap-to-hear button (ElevenLabs voice matched to npc_voice_id)
Auto-end after 8 user turns OR user types 'klaar'/'done'/'einde'
Transcript persisted to roleplay_sessions.transcript_json
Sticky end-conversation button always visible
Streaming via @ai-sdk/react useChat() hook

stories.title: Roleplay grading + 5-rubric scorecard
stories.ears: When a roleplay conversation ends, the server shall call Claude with a 5-rubric grading prompt + transcript + scenario success criteria. The system shall render a scorecard with star ratings + errors + XP + badge.
acceptance: Server function /api/roleplay/:session-id/grade calls Claude (generateObject + Zod) with the 5-rubric structured output
Rubric: grammar (1-5), vocabulary (1-5), task-completion (1-5), fluency (1-5), politeness (1-5). Overall stars = round(avg).
Returns error list: { error_type, example_nl, correction, concept_slug } persisted to roleplay_errors
/app/scenario/:slug/scorecard route renders the breakdown + XP earned (scaled by stars) + badge if unlocked
Best-attempt model: re-doing replaces only if higher score
Retry button -> re-enters scenario
Continue button -> next unit

stories.title: Errors -> spaced repetition queue (SM-2)
stories.ears: When roleplay errors are recorded, the system shall enqueue corresponding drill cards in spaced_rep_queue with SM-2 scheduling. When a user starts a lesson, the system shall surface up to 3 due review cards before the new lesson's content.
acceptance: spaced_rep_queue rows: user_id, item_type, item_id, due_at, ease (start 2.5), interval, last_seen, last_correct
On each user lesson start: query due_at <= now() LIMIT 3 -> surface as 'Review' section
On review answer: SM-2 update (ease+0.1 if correct, ease-0.2 if wrong; interval *= ease)
Errors from drill mistakes ALSO queue (not just roleplay)
Cap: max 10 active review items per user at any time (prevent backlog burnout)

stories.title: XP + streak engine
stories.ears: When a user completes a lesson, the system shall award 20 XP + 5 coins. When the user completes a boss-fight, the system shall award 100 XP × (stars/5) + 25 coins. Streak counter increments on first daily completion.
acceptance: xp_events table populated per award with (user_id, source, source_id, amount, at)
coin_events table populated similarly
daily_completions row created on first lesson of the day
users.streak_days increments daily; resets on miss unless freeze auto-consumed
Top app bar shows: 🔥 streak · ⚡ xp · 🪙 coins (server-component, fresh on every page load)
Streak reset cron at midnight user-local-time: check yesterday's daily_completions; if missing and no freeze, reset streak

stories.title: Coins shop (/app/shop)
stories.ears: When a user navigates to /app/shop, the system shall show purchasable items: streak freeze (50 coins), drill hint pack (10 coins each).
acceptance: Two items in v0: streak freeze (50🪙, increments freeze balance), hint (10🪙, increments hint balance)
Purchase server function deducts coins + grants item (transactional)
Disabled if balance < cost
coin_events records the spend
UI shows balance prominently
Phase 2 stretch: cosmetic mascot outfits

stories.title: Streak freezes (auto-consume + milestone awards)
stories.ears: When a user misses a day, the system shall consume one streak freeze if available; otherwise reset the streak. When a user hits 7/14/30/60/100-day milestone, the system shall auto-award a freeze.
acceptance: Daily midnight cron checks for missed days per user; consumes from users.streak_freezes_balance
Milestone awards: 7d=+1, 14d=+1, 30d=+2, 60d=+3, 100d=+5
UI shows freeze balance + 'Use freeze' indicator on streak counter when freezes available
Streak-broken email (US-028) only fires when freeze was NOT available

stories.title: Badge system (15 v0 badges)
stories.ears: When a user crosses a milestone, the system shall auto-award the matching badge. Badges show on the profile page.
acceptance: badges table seeded with 15 v0 badges (first-lesson, 7-day-streak, 30-day-streak, 100-day-streak, first-boss-fight, perfect-roleplay-5-stars, all-a2-unit-1-complete, all-a2-unit-2-complete, ..., a2-complete, vocab-master-100, vocab-master-500, early-bird, night-owl, weekend-warrior, comeback-kid)
Event-driven award check: after every xp_event or lesson completion, run badge eligibility check
Badge unlock UI: full-screen Stroop celebration on award
user_badges table populated
Profile shows badges grid, locked badges greyed-out

stories.title: Global leaderboard (/app/leaderboard)
stories.ears: When a user navigates to /app/leaderboard, the system shall show three tabs: Today / This Week / All Time. Each shows the top 50 users by XP in that window with the current user highlighted.
acceptance: Server-function queries xp_events with date filters
Today = since 00:00 user-tz; This Week = since Monday user-tz; All-Time = total users.xp_total
Top 50 per tab; current user shown even if outside top 50 (separate row)
Per-row: display_name, avatar, xp, current_streak, level (Bronze/Silver/Gold/Platinum)
Tap user -> /app/profile/:displayName
Refresh on view (no live socket for v0)

stories.title: Public profile page (/app/profile/:displayName)
stories.ears: When any user views a profile by displayName, the system shall show that user's stats: avatar, level, total XP, current streak, badges grid, unit progress chips.
acceptance: Server function returns: user(public fields) + badges_earned + user_unit_progress
Level derived from xp_total: <1000=Bronze, <5000=Silver, <20000=Gold, >=20000=Platinum
Badge grid: 15 badges, earned ones in colour, unearned greyed
Unit progress: visual chips per unit (completed=green, in-progress=orange, locked=grey)
Public by default; no privacy controls in v0

stories.title: Users directory (/app/users)
stories.ears: When a signed-in user navigates to /app/users, the system shall show a browseable list of all signed-up users, sortable by XP / streak / level.
acceptance: Server function paginates users by chosen sort
Sortable columns: XP, streak, level, joined-date
Each row: avatar, display name, level, streak, xp
Tap row -> /app/profile/:displayName

stories.title: Web push daily nag (CF scheduled worker)
stories.ears: When the daily cron runs at a user's reminder_hour, the system shall send a web push notification if the user hasn't completed today's lesson.
acceptance: wrangler.jsonc adds cron trigger: 'every hour at minute 0'
scheduled handler in src/server/cron.ts: query users where reminder_hour === current_local_hour AND no daily_completion today AND reminder_enabled
For each: send Web Push (via VAPID keys) to all of user's push_subscriptions
Notification copy: '🔥 5-min lesson keeps your X-day streak alive!' (varies by streak length)
Click opens /app/path
VAPID keys generated once via wrangler secret put VAPID_PUBLIC + VAPID_PRIVATE
Stale subscriptions (HTTP 410) auto-removed from push_subscriptions

stories.title: Email: weekly digest + streak-broken recovery
stories.ears: When Sunday 10am cron runs, the system shall send each user a weekly digest email. When a user's streak breaks, the system shall send a one-time recovery email the next day.
acceptance: Sunday cron: send digest via Resend API with this-week XP, leaderboard rank, badges earned, units completed
Streak-broken cron (daily): for each user whose streak reset yesterday + had streak >= 3, send recovery email
Both emails use Resend templates with Stroop branding
notification_log table tracks email sends to prevent double-send
From: noreply@lekkertaal.ronanconnolly.dev (sender domain on Resend)

stories.title: ElevenLabs TTS + R2 cache
stories.ears: When any Dutch text in the UI is tapped on its speaker icon, the system shall play ElevenLabs-synthesised audio. The audio is cached in R2 by content hash to avoid re-charging.
acceptance: Server function /api/tts: input {text, voice_id}, output audio/mpeg
R2 key format: tts/{voice_id}/{sha256(text)}.mp3
On hit: stream from R2 (free)
On miss: call ElevenLabs API, store to R2, then stream
Falls back to OpenAI TTS (alloy / nova voices) if ELEVENLABS_API_KEY missing OR ElevenLabs returns 5xx
wrangler.jsonc binds R2 bucket lekkertaal-tts
Client <Speaker> component plays via <audio src='/api/tts?text=…&voice=…'>
Prefetch on hover for snappier UX

stories.title: Cutesy theme: Fredoka + Nunito + Dutch palette + chunky buttons
stories.ears: When the app loads, the system shall apply Fredoka for display headings + Nunito for body, the Dutch palette tokens, rounded-3xl card radius, chunky 3D button shadows, and Motion whileTap on interactive buttons.
acceptance: Tailwind config loads Fredoka (display) + Nunito (body) via Google Fonts
CSS variables: --color-primary #FF6B1A, --color-secondary #1F6FB2, --color-accent #FF7AA2, --color-paper #FFF8EE, --color-ink #1A1A1A
Light + dark mode both styled (dark = warm-near-black bg)
shadcn button override: rounded-2xl, box-shadow: 0 4px 0 0 var(--color-primary-dark), translateY-1px on hover, translateY-4px on press (chunky 3D)
Motion `whileTap={{ scale: 0.95 }}` on all primary CTAs
Cards use rounded-3xl
Storybook-ish theme demo at /dev/theme route (gitignored, dev-only)

stories.title: Stroop the stroopwafel mascot (Rive)
stories.ears: When events happen (lesson complete, streak milestone, streak at risk, long inactivity), the system shall show Stroop with the matching emotional state via Rive state machine.
acceptance: Generate Stroop concept art via Nano Banana 2 (/ro:generate-image) — 3 sketches, pick one
Author Rive file (.riv) with state machine: idle, happy (lesson complete), proud (streak milestone), concerned (streak at risk), sleeping (24h+ inactive)
If Rive authoring is too slow tonight: fall back to a static SVG Stroop per state
<Stroop state='happy' /> component reads the right state
Appears on: /app/path (idle/concerned), lesson-complete (happy), boss-fight scorecard (proud), settings (sleeping if no activity)
Lazy-loaded via React.lazy + Suspense

stories.title: 4 hero sound effects
stories.ears: When lesson events trigger (correct, wrong, lesson complete, streak fire), the system shall play a short SFX.
acceptance: Generate 4 SFX via /ro:sfx-elevenlabs: correct-ding (200ms), wrong-buzz (150ms), lesson-complete fanfare (1.5s), streak-fire whoosh (600ms)
Stored in public/sfx/*.mp3
Triggered on: drill correct/wrong, lesson complete, streak milestone
Mute toggle in /app/settings (users.sfx_enabled column)
Default off on mobile to respect data, on on desktop

stories.title: PWA manifest + service worker
stories.ears: When a user visits the app on supported devices, the system shall be installable as a PWA. The service worker shall cache the shell + fonts + Stroop assets and handle web push.
acceptance: vite-plugin-pwa configured with manifest: name='Lekkertaal', short_name='Lekkertaal', icons (192/512), theme_color=#FF6B1A, background_color=#FFF8EE
Service worker caches: app shell, Fredoka+Nunito fonts, /public/sfx/*, /public/stroop/*
Cache strategy: NetworkFirst for /api/*, CacheFirst for static
Web Push handler in service worker registers VAPID + displays notifications
iOS-friendly install prompt component (visible only on iOS Safari)

stories.title: Production deploy to lekkertaal.ronanconnolly.dev
stories.ears: When pnpm deploy runs (or main branch merges), the system shall deploy to Cloudflare Workers under lekkertaal.ronanconnolly.dev.
acceptance: wrangler.jsonc routes/domains configured for lekkertaal.ronanconnolly.dev

All secrets pushed via wrangler secret put: CLERK_SECRET_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY, ELEVENLABS_API_KEY, RESEND_API_KEY, SENTRY_DSN, POSTHOG_PROJECT_KEY, VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY, CLERK_WEBHOOK_SECRET
D1 binding wired with production database_id
R2 bucket wired (lekkertaal-tts)
DNS record added via Cloudflare API (CNAME lekkertaal -> worker, orange-cloud proxied)
Smoke test: curl https://lekkertaal.ronanconnolly.dev returns 200 and HTML
REQUIRES ronanconnolly.dev CF account token (CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN_RONAN). If missing, story stops with explicit message to user.

stories.title: Seed 4 Yellowtail Dutch Group users + simulated history
stories.ears: When pnpm seed:users runs, the system shall create 4 users (Ronan, Mehmet, Judith, Michiel) with realistic starting state so leaderboard is non-empty on day one.
acceptance: seed/users.json: 4 users with placeholder emails (replace at runtime), CEFR level A2, varied progress
Ronan: 14-day streak, all Unit 1-3 complete, on Unit 4
Mehmet: 21-day streak, all Unit 1-3 complete, on Unit 4 ahead
Judith: 14-day streak, all Unit 1-3 complete, on Unit 4
Michiel: 7-day streak, all Unit 1-5 complete (he's the teacher), on Unit 6
All with simulated xp_events backdated to look natural
Idempotent re-run; skips if user already exists by displayName

phases.title: Phase 2 — Foundation+ (post-v0 ~30 stories)
phases.goal: Speech in/out, A1+B1 content, social features, content authoring readiness, league system trigger when user base grows.

stories.title: STT via Whisper for speaking drills
stories.ears: When a user is in a drill with speak-mode enabled, the system shall record audio + transcribe via Whisper API.

stories.title: Pronunciation scoring (phoneme-level)
stories.ears: When the user speaks during a drill, the system shall compare phoneme-level match against the canonical and return a pronunciation score 1-5.

stories.title: A1 starter content (10 units)
stories.ears: When an A1 user signs up, the system shall have a full A1 curriculum with units, lessons, vocab, exercises, and 6 boss-fight scenarios.

stories.title: B1 starter content (10 units)
stories.ears: When a B1 user signs up, the system shall have a full B1 curriculum.

stories.title: Friends/follow graph
stories.ears: When user A taps Follow on user B's profile, the system shall create a follow record. Followers + following counts shown.

stories.title: Friend-only leaderboard tab
stories.ears: When a user has at least one friend, the system shall add a Friends tab to the leaderboard.

stories.title: Peer drills: 'send to a friend'
stories.ears: When user A finishes a drill, the system shall offer 'send this challenge to a friend'.

stories.title: User-suggested scenarios + moderation queue
stories.ears: When a user submits a scenario via a form, the system shall queue it for admin approval.

stories.title: Admin moderation UI (/admin/scenarios)
stories.ears: When an admin views the moderation page, the system shall list pending scenarios + accept/reject buttons.

stories.title: League system (10-tier weekly)
stories.ears: When the user base exceeds 30 active users, the system shall enable the league system: 10 tiers, weekly promotion of top 25%, demotion of bottom 25%.

stories.title: More badges (50+ total)
stories.ears: When milestones are hit, the system shall award badges from an expanded catalog.

stories.title: Daily quests system
stories.ears: When a user opens the app, the system shall show 3 daily quests with bonus XP/coin rewards.

stories.title: Hearts mechanic (A/B test gated)
stories.ears: When the hearts feature flag is on, the system shall enable Duolingo-style hearts.

stories.title: Story mode: longer narrative scenarios
stories.ears: When a user enters story mode, the system shall present multi-scene narrative scenarios (~20 min each).

stories.title: Customizable mascot outfits
stories.ears: When a user purchases a mascot outfit, the system shall display Stroop with that outfit.

stories.title: Achievement timeline visualization
stories.ears: When a user views their profile, the system shall show a timeline of milestones.

stories.title: Streak history calendar view
stories.ears: When a user views their profile, the system shall show a GitHub-style calendar of daily activity.

stories.title: Push notification A/B testing
stories.ears: When the daily nag fires, the system shall A/B test copy variants for click-through.

stories.title: Email: more transactional templates
stories.ears: When milestones / re-engagement triggers fire, the system shall send branded emails.

stories.title: Drill: image-based vocab
stories.ears: When an image drill loads, the system shall show an image and ask user to pick or type the Dutch word.

stories.title: Drill: conjugation table fill
stories.ears: When a conjugation drill loads, the system shall show a verb table with cells to fill.

stories.title: Spaced rep algorithm tuning + reporting
stories.ears: When SM-2 has 30 days of data, the system shall surface per-user retention stats + tune ease per-user.

stories.title: Power-user CSV export of progress
stories.ears: When a user requests an export, the system shall email a CSV of their XP/streak/badge history.

stories.title: Privacy settings
stories.ears: When a user opens settings, the system shall let them hide profile from leaderboard, hide xp on profile, etc.

stories.title: Account deletion flow
stories.ears: When a user requests account deletion, the system shall delete all PII + content within 30 days.

stories.title: Search across vocab
stories.ears: When a user searches a Dutch word, the system shall find it in vocab + show example uses + audio.

stories.title: Grammar reference index
stories.ears: When a user opens /app/grammar, the system shall show a browseable index of all grammar concepts.

stories.title: Boss-fight retry with errors-from-last-attempt prefilled
stories.ears: When a user retries a boss-fight, the system shall surface their previous-attempt errors as 'watch out for' tips.

stories.title: Mobile (Expo wrapper)
stories.ears: When a user installs the iOS/Android app, the system shall provide a native shell wrapping the PWA via Expo or Capacitor.

stories.title: Push: time-zone-aware smart reminders
stories.ears: When a user has consistent activity pattern, the system shall learn their best reminder hour.

phases.title: Phase 3 — Polish & Growth (~20 stories)
phases.goal: UI polish, design refinement, animations, perf, accessibility, marketing site.

stories.title: A/B testing infrastructure via PostHog flags
stories.ears: When the team wants to A/B test a feature, the system shall route via PostHog flags.

stories.title: Onboarding optimization (track + iterate funnel)
stories.ears: When users sign up, the system shall track funnel events; team iterates on drop-off points.

stories.title: Lesson celebration animation polish
stories.ears: When a user completes a lesson, Motion-driven celebration animation with confetti + Stroop dance.

stories.title: Loading states + skeleton screens everywhere
stories.ears: When any data is loading, the system shall show a Stroop-themed skeleton.

stories.title: Error states + empty states designed
stories.ears: When errors or empty data occur, the system shall show on-brand empty/error illustrations.

stories.title: Accessibility audit (WCAG AA)
stories.ears: Every interactive element shall be keyboard-navigable, screen-reader-labelled, and meet contrast AA.

stories.title: Performance audit (Lighthouse > 90 PWA score)
stories.ears: Lighthouse PWA + Performance scores >= 90 on mobile.

stories.title: SEO optimization for marketing pages
stories.ears: Marketing pages have proper meta tags, OG images, structured data.

stories.title: Marketing landing site
stories.ears: When a non-signed-in user lands, the system shall show a marketing site explaining the product.

stories.title: Social share images (per-badge, per-streak-milestone)
stories.ears: When a user unlocks a badge, the system shall generate a shareable image via Nano Banana 2.

stories.title: Referral system (invite + bonus coins)
stories.ears: When a user refers a friend, both get bonus coins on the friend's sign-up.

stories.title: Daily quest design pass
stories.ears: Quest variety expanded with thoughtful difficulty curves.

stories.title: Profile customization: bio + avatar pick
stories.ears: Users can write a bio + pick from preset avatars.

stories.title: Mascot animations during long roleplays
stories.ears: Stroop appears intermittently during roleplay to nudge user (rare, ~once per 10 turns).

stories.title: Sentry alerts wired to Slack/email
stories.ears: When error rate > threshold, alert fires.

stories.title: Cron job monitoring (alerts on miss)
stories.ears: When daily/weekly cron misses its window, alert fires.

stories.title: User analytics dashboard (internal)
stories.ears: Internal /admin/analytics shows MAU, retention, conversion, etc.

stories.title: Bug-report-in-app button
stories.ears: When users hit a bug button, system collects context + opens ticket.

stories.title: Help center / FAQ page
stories.ears: Help center with FAQ entries.

stories.title: Settings: theme picker (light/dark/auto)
stories.ears: User can pick theme; system respects.

phases.title: Phase 4 — Scale (~15 stories)
phases.goal: Monetization, multi-language source, content authoring, cohort feature, enterprise readiness.

stories.title: Monetization: Stripe + Super tier
stories.ears: When a user wants premium features, the system shall offer a 'Super' subscription via Stripe.

stories.title: Multi-locale UI: Dutch labels alongside English
stories.ears: When a user picks Dutch UI, the system shall display all UI in Dutch.

stories.title: Pivot to other languages (Spanish, French, German)
stories.ears: When the company adds Spanish/French/German, the system shall support multi-target-language curricula.

stories.title: AI-improvised scenarios (free-play mode)
stories.ears: When a user types a scenario topic, AI generates a scenario live.

stories.title: Content authoring UI for teachers
stories.ears: When a teacher signs up, the system shall offer a content authoring flow for their own curriculum.

stories.title: Cohort/classroom mode
stories.ears: When a teacher creates a cohort, the system shall support cohort-scoped users + leaderboard + progress.

stories.title: Real-time voice via OpenAI Realtime API
stories.ears: When a user enters voice roleplay, AI speaks back via Realtime API.

stories.title: Story mode v2: choose-your-own-adventure
stories.ears: When a user enters story mode, branching narrative with choices.

stories.title: Group chat / classmate practice rooms
stories.ears: When friends want to chat in Dutch, the system shall provide moderated practice rooms.

stories.title: Custom scenario builder for power users
stories.ears: When a power user wants their own scenario, the system shall offer a scenario-building UI.

stories.title: API for external integrations
stories.ears: When external apps want to integrate, the system shall offer a public API.

stories.title: Admin dashboard for support
stories.ears: When support staff need to help users, the system shall offer an admin dashboard.

stories.title: Analytics for self-improvement
stories.ears: When a user views their stats, the system shall show learning-velocity charts.

stories.title: Native iOS / Android apps
stories.ears: When a user installs the iOS/Android app, the system shall provide a native experience.

stories.title: Enterprise features: SSO, audit logs, RBAC
stories.ears: When an enterprise buys, the system shall offer SAML SSO + audit logs + RBAC.
